Solution:

\text{Let } \alpha = \sin^{-1}\left(-\frac{4}{5}\right).

\text{Based on the domain of } \sin^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 9, \quad y = -12, \quad r = 15

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{4}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = -\sqrt{\frac{1 - \left(\frac{3}{5}\right)}{2}}

\sin\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = -\frac{\sqrt{5}}{5}

Solution:

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 13, \quad y = 8, \quad r = \sqrt{233}

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{8 \, \sqrt{233}}{233}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{13 \, \sqrt{233}}{233}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{13 \, \sqrt{233}}{233}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{\sqrt{466} \sqrt{13 \, \sqrt{233} + 233}}{466}

\text{Based on the domain of } \sec^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } II.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = -13, \quad y = 3, \quad r = \sqrt{178}

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{3 \, \sqrt{178}}{178}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = -\frac{13 \, \sqrt{178}}{178}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(-\frac{13 \, \sqrt{178}}{178}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{\sqrt{89} \sqrt{{\left| -13 \, \sqrt{178} + 178 \right|}}}{178}

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 4, \quad y = 3, \quad r = 5

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{3}{5}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{4}{5}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{4}{5}\right)}{\frac{3}{5}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1}{3}

\text{Based on the domain of } \csc^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 14, \quad y = -6, \quad r = 2 \, \sqrt{58}

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{3 \, \sqrt{58}}{58}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{7 \, \sqrt{58}}{58}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{7 \, \sqrt{58}}{58}\right)}{-\frac{3 \, \sqrt{58}}{58}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = -\frac{\sqrt{58} {\left| 7 \, \sqrt{58} - 58 \right|}}{174}

\text{Based on the domain of } \tan^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 6, \quad y = 11, \quad r = \sqrt{157}

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{11 \, \sqrt{157}}{157}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{6 \, \sqrt{157}}{157}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{6 \, \sqrt{157}}{157}\right)}{\frac{11 \, \sqrt{157}}{157}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{\sqrt{157} {\left| 6 \, \sqrt{157} - 157 \right|}}{1727}

\text{Based on the domain of } \tan^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 10, \quad y = -12, \quad r = 2 \, \sqrt{61}

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = -\frac{6 \, \sqrt{61}}{61}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{5 \, \sqrt{61}}{61}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{5 \, \sqrt{61}}{61}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{\sqrt{122} \sqrt{5 \, \sqrt{61} + 61}}{122}

\text{Let } \alpha = \csc^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{137}}{11}\right).

\text{Based on the domain of } \csc^{-1} \text{ and the argument, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I.

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 4, \quad y = 11, \quad r = \sqrt{137}

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{11 \, \sqrt{137}}{137}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{4 \, \sqrt{137}}{137}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \pm\sqrt{\frac{1+\cos(\alpha)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = +\sqrt{\frac{1 + \left(\frac{4 \, \sqrt{137}}{137}\right)}{2}}

\cos\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{\sqrt{274} \sqrt{4 \, \sqrt{137} + 137}}{274}

\text{Let } \alpha = \csc^{-1}\left(\sqrt{2}\right).

\text{Therefore, the half-angle } \frac{\alpha}{2} \text{ must be in Quadrant } I.

\text{Using the reference triangle, we identify: } x = 6, \quad y = 6, \quad r = 6 \, \sqrt{2}

\text{Evaluate the required fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}

\text{Apply the half-angle identity (choosing the sign based on the half-angle quadrant):}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1-\cos(\alpha)}{\sin(\alpha)}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{1 - \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\right)}{\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}}

\tan\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right) = \frac{\sqrt{2} {\left| \sqrt{2} - 2 \right|}}{2}
